chunk_summary: Pediatric MASLD screening in India emphasizes ALT and ultrasound for obese/overweight children, with tailored guidelines addressing local prevalence and risk factors.
learning_objectives: Understand the updated MASLD terminology and its relevance in pediatric metabolic risk.
Learn the 2024 ISPGHAN recommendations for MASLD screening in Indian children.
Compare ALT and ultrasound as screening tools for pediatric MASLD.
Recognize the importance of systematic screening in high-risk pediatric populations.
key_facts: MASLD replaces NAFLD under the steatotic liver disease (SLD) umbrella, with MASH replacing NASH.
ALT thresholds: >22 U/L for girls, >26 U/L for boys; >80 U/L indicates higher concern.
2017 NASPGHAN guidelines recommend ALT as the primary screening test, not routine ultrasound.
2024 ISPGHAN guidelines for India recommend screening obese (BMI ≥95th percentile) and overweight (BMI ≥85th to <95th percentile) children with additional risk factors using ALT and ultrasound.
Persistent ALT >2× upper limit of normal for >3 months warrants further evaluation.
reference_quote: The 2024 ISPGHAN pediatric MASLD practice recommendations, tailored to Indian context, advise screening obese children (BMI ≥95th percentile) and overweight children (BMI ≥85th to <95th percentile) with additional risk factors using abdominal ultrasound in combination with ALT.
